item.title: Calming games
item.description: I enjoy playing No Man's Sky. I know it doesn't have the greatest story ever, but it's relaxing to just drift thru a virtual galaxy discovering new and strange things without a pressing goal.
item.dc:creator: Othagovna

item.title: Exemplary in a lost world
item.description: In one of the most notable moments in sports history, Kenyan runner Abel Mutai was just a few feet from the finish line, but became confused with the signage and stopped thinking he had completed the race. A Spanish athlete, Ivan Fernandez, was right behind him, and after realizing what was happening, he started shouting at the Kenyan for him to continue running; but Mutai didn't understand his Spanish. Fernandez eventually caught up to him and instead of passing him, he pushed him to victory. A journalist asked Ivan, "Why did you do that?" Ivan replied, “My dream is that someday we can have a kind of community life where we push and help each other to win.” The journalist insisted “But why did you let the Kenyan win?" Ivan replied, "I didn't let him win, he was going to win.” The journalist insisted again, “But you could have won!”Ivan looked at him & replied, “But what would be the merit of my victory? What would be the honor of that medal? What would my Mom think of that?”
item.dc:creator: Zergcollective

item.title: I used a drone to take a picture of present-day Hiroshima to visualize the size and power of the atomic bomb dropped 79 years ago today
item.description:
Essentially everything in this photo, from left edge to right edge, that wasn't made of concrete and steel was knocked flat. Everything else in the city was set on fire by the heat. The fireball did not touch the ground, so the common idea that people or buildings were "vaporized" is a misconception.
While the Genbaku Dome (Atomic Bomb Dome) and the adjacent park are the de facto memorials to the bombing, the actual hypocenter of the bomb is marked by a single stone block and plaque a few blocks east of the park. It is very innocuous and out of the way.
Close-up of the hypocenter monument.
Originally the Hiroshima Prefectural Industrial Promotion Hall, and now commonly called the Genbaku Dome, Atomic Bomb Dome or A-Bomb Dome (原爆ドーム, Genbaku Dōmu), is part of the Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park in Hiroshima, Japan and was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1996. It a protected monument, and the city has ordinances to protect the sightlines of the park from advertisements and signage.
"Human Shadow Etched in Stone" These stone steps were at the entrance to the Hiroshima Branch of the Sumitomo Bank. Exposed to the atomic explosion the person sitting on the steps waiting for the bank to open is thought to have died on the spot with no possibility of escape. The intense heat of the A-bomb turned the steps whitish; the stone under the sitting person remained dark, like a shadow. The bank is about 1,350 feet from the hypocenter, and this phenomena is often confused with "people being vaporized, only leaving behind a shadow." What was more commonly seen in both Hiroshima and Nagasaki was that materials in shadow against the bomb retained their original color, while the areas outside of shadow were scorched or bleached. I will post more examples of this from Nagasaki in a few days.
Children's Peace Monument within the memorial park. This is a statue of Sadako Sasaki, whose story and her connection to folding paper cranes while she died of leukemia about 10 years after the bombing is a lasting symbol of peace. The cases behind the statue are filled with thousands of paper cranes on strings.
Statue of Sadako upholding an origami crane.
Sadako's original sandals and a personal bag that she carried with her.
Sadako's original paper cranes, made from whatever she could get her hands on while dying in a hospital of cancer caused by nuclear fallout. It never occurred to me that these cranes still existed, or that I would ever see them in person. They are so tiny and delicate and beautiful.
A paper peace crate stuck into the back of the lava rock obelisk at the Trinity Site in New Mexico, which I photographed last year.
Dogtag of James Ryan, an American B-24 crew member who was shot down near Hiroshima in Spring of 1945, and who would die during the atomic bombing in a Japanese POW camp.
Backside of the Atomic Bomb Dome. This is the side that faced the blast, and you can tell that the only remaining pieces of the building are the innermost concrete core; everything facing the explosion was completely shattered and blown away.
A view from above and behind the Dome, about half as high as the fireball would have been. This is a composite image where I tried to remove a protective mesh that obscured the view of the dome. In the upper right is part of the T-shaped bridge that was the intended target of the bomb.
The Peace Memorial itself, with the eternal flame of peace. The flame at the top was lit on August 1, 1964, and has been burning ever since in protest of nuclear weapons, and will continue to burn until there are no nuclear weapons left on earth. I also visited Nagasaki on this trip and I will be posting images from that in a few days. If you are into this stuff, I post way more regularly on my socials under @AtomicAerials as opposed to the seasonal or OTD dumps I make here on Imgur. I'd really enjoy having more people to share this with who would also like to share their thoughts and experiences with me.
item.dc:creator: AtomicAerials

item.title: IT WAS ALL LIES!
item.description: My cereal was NOT part of a balanced breakfast. I CANNOT do anything I set my mind to. Prices have NOT gone down due to advanced technology. Going to University did NOT guarantee me a job. I was NOT able to get into a good union. And worst of all...there are NO flying cars!

item.title: Deviousness Day 128
item.description:
Welcome to another Deviousness week! I am working on a bunch this week for the deviousness server and will be making a server announcement with the changes aiming for friday yay! I has a Deviousness discord server! We have a general area for memes and discussions of all different SFWish topics! We have gaming fun! Right now we have a 7 Days to Die casual server with no horde nights to explore the full release! We also do Helldivers 2 friday nights. All are welcome who want to game or watch us game or just hang out feel free to hop into the vc. please do be respectful if we are in game let us chat for the game. We have an opt in kink area for dirtier conversations and then a role locked area for deeper kink discussions! (It is role locked because we take privacy and security very seriously and the discord is a place with real people sharing and things being shared is a privilege) Thank you to everyone who has joined and made it such a unique and special place! Deviousness discord that is 18+: https://discord.gg/deviousness Deviousness steam group: https://steamcommunity.com/groups/DeviousnessDiscord
The above image was made by the wonderful CammyJay! They have their own channel in the Deviousness discord where they share artwork and on fridays do drawings based on word prompts given by members. They will also be running a fun new area soon with a monthly photo contest voted on by the community where the winner will receive a custom digital artwork of their photo the following week by Cammyjay! Deviousness discord that is 18+: https://discord.gg/deviousness I was asked to help promote a fellow imgurian's twitch stream and cat surgery fundraiser @ElbowDeepInPackages Twitch: https://www.twitch.tv/erosrogziel last update: https://imgur.com/a/f4HcNvV The consult appointment was on July 9th, at that alone costed $180. She's up to $504 as of Sunday night, but that still leaves her far short of the expected costs. There will be additional costs for the procedure itself: Ko-FI: https://ko-fi.com/erosrogziel As a fellow imgurian who has asked for help when my own kitty was distressed I hope the community can halps them too!
I love you *my* Daddy. Friendly reminder I am claimed so I would appreciate keeping that in mind when making comments towards me see dis: https://imgur.com/JipV5UR I has a post with links to all my past posts. enjoy: https://imgur.com/a/ZDA2CBe I am changing how I add people to my ping list so please see here to get added or removed: https://imgur.com/gallery/fFYOR2B
item.dc:creator: PotatoCheesMonk

item.title: Adopt Codsworth not Dall-e
item.description: #fallout4 EDIT The comments were interesting to read! And very informative (sorry for using Drake). So I thought I'll add some info. I researched the AI image generators and found some concerning facts that some of you might not know about. 1. The models were trained using copyrighted artwork and potentially any image YOU have ever posted online of yourself, your house or your cat. This was done by data mining and by using a legal loophole: "for research purposes". Unfortunately, this "research" quickly became a commercial product. If it exists online, AI has probably seen it. 2. You could write a prompt containing any artists name and it will generate an image with the same style. Legal fraud! 3. AI models are unable to forget. So all new generations of AI models will have your data. Forever! 4. There's nothing we can do about it. Corporations say the last word. 5. It's very unethical and should be regulated. Artists spend days and weeks on their work. You're not an artist for writing a prompt. Boycott AI imaging.
item.dc:creator: RizunaAnTheBun
